confirmed_fact_templates.text: Trace data confirms the request or entity entered an observable service flow.
No matching trace was found for the provided incident locator.
A related persisted record exists in the database.
At least one expected persisted record is missing.
A related cache or idempotency key exists.
The related cache key is absent.
The related cache key still has a positive TTL.
An expected trace span is missing from the observed flow.

rules.root_cause: The request was most likely short-circuited by cache or idempotency state before the expected side effect executed.
alternative_hypotheses: The task dispatch step may be missing from the code path for this domain variant.
recommended_next_actions: Check which workflow wrote the idempotency or cache key.
Verify whether retry or guard logic pre-created the key.
If operationally safe, clear the stale key and replay the flow manually.

rules.root_cause: The request could not be confirmed in trace data and did not reach the expected persisted state.
alternative_hypotheses: Trace collection may be incomplete even if the request briefly reached the application.
recommended_next_actions: Confirm whether tracing is sampled, missing, or misconfigured for this entrypoint.
Check ingress and application logs around the incident window.
Verify whether the request was rejected before application code executed.

rules.root_cause: The request appears to have stopped before the expected persistence step completed.
alternative_hypotheses: A domain validation rule may have rejected the request before insert.
recommended_next_actions: Inspect validation and pre-persistence guard logic.
Check whether the request was rejected by business rules before insert.
Add a more specific runbook branch for this domain flow.

fallback.root_cause: The available evidence is insufficient to isolate a single root cause.
alternative_hypotheses: The missing signal may exist in MQ or log data that is not part of the MVP fixture set.
recommended_next_actions: Collect one more discriminating signal such as MQ or log evidence.
Narrow the incident window and retry the investigation.
Add a more specialized runbook for this symptom pattern.
